seo.title: Building Customer Support with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with Claude's tool use enables powerful RAG-driven interactions with CRM integrations, knowledge base retrieval, and automated ticket handling. However, tool chains can cascade errors, produce conflicting results, or deviate from policy-compliant responses, risking customer trust and compliance violations. Vex provides essential verification to ensure response accuracy, PII safety, tone consistency, and proper escalations.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on a Claude model handling user queries via tool use. Incoming support tickets trigger parallel tool calls: one for knowledge base vector search (RAG), another for CRM order lookup, and optionally ticket creation. Claude reasons over results to generate empathetic, accurate responses while checking escalation triggers like refund requests over $500.

Tools are defined for 'search_kb', 'lookup_order', and 'create_ticket', invoked via messages.create() with tool_use enabled. The agent maintains conversation state, filtering PII from responses and ensuring brand tone. Failures like conflicting order data or hallucinated escalations are common without verification.

Vex wraps the tool use response, verifying against schemas for accuracy, policy compliance, and safety before customer-facing output.

risks.name: Cascading Tool Failures
risks.description: Early KB search failure causes order lookup to use stale data, leading to incorrect ticket resolutions
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool chain integrity and response consistency against expected schemas

risks.name: Conflicting Parallel Results
risks.description: Order lookup returns different customer data than KB profile, causing inconsistent responses
risks.mitigation: Vex detects conflicts and triggers human review or resolution logic

risks.name: PII Leakage in Responses
risks.description: Tool results containing credit card numbers or emails passed directly to customers
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering redacts sensitive data before response delivery

risks.name: Incorrect Escalation Triggers
risks.description: Agent misses high-value refund requests requiring supervisor escalation
risks.mitigation: Vex validates escalation logic against policy rules and monetary thresholds

implementation.title: Define Customer Support Tools
implementation.description: Create tool definitions for KB search, order lookup, and ticket creation using Anthropic's tool schema

implementation.title: Basic Tool Use Loop
implementation.description: Implement the core agent loop handling parallel tool calls and response generation
implementation.code:
client = anthropic.Anthropic()

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=tools,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Customer #12345 can't track order ABC789"}]
)

while message.stop_reason == "tool_use":
    for tool in message.tool_use.contents:
        if tool.name == "lookup_order":
            result = crm.lookup_order(tool.input['order_id'])
        # Execute other tools...
    
    message = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
        max_tokens=1024,
        tools=tools,
        messages=message.content + [{"role": "user", "content": tool_results}],
        # Pass previous message ID for conversation
    )
